summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that any authenticated user can create a webhook with an arbitrary internal target URL (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8888/). When a task event fires, the Vikunja server sends a POST request containing user PII, project data, and task content to the attacker-controlled URL, with no SSRF protections.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the Vikunja server to make an HTTP POST request to a callback listener on 127.0.0.1:8888, proving the server can reach arbitrary internal endpoints. The callback payload should contain the proof marker string and sensitive task/user data.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction built Vikunja from source, started the server, registered a user, created a webhook targeting http://127.0.0.1:8888/EVOSHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_MARKER_d4e7f1a3, triggered it via task creation, and the Go callback server received a POST request containing the full webhook payload (user PII, project data, task content) at the proof marker path.
verification_method: The exploit creates a unique proof marker path (EVOSHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_MARKER_d4e7f1a3) in the webhook target URL. A Go HTTP callback server writes the received request (method, path, headers, body) to /tmp/ssrf_proof.txt. The exploit script polls for this file for up to 30 seconds. Success is determined by the presence of this file containing the proof marker path, which distinguishes a genuine SSRF callback from unrelated server startup traffic.
evidence_produced_by_run: The Go callback server received a POST request to /EVOSHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_MARKER_d4e7f1a3 from the Vikunja server process.
The webhook payload contained JSON with event_name=task.created, full user object (username=attacker), project details, and task content.
The callback was received within 1 second of task creation, confirming the SSRF is reliable and synchronous enough to exploit.
Server logs confirmed the webhook was created with target_url=http://127.0.0.1:8888/EVOSHUNT_SSRF_PROOF_MARKER_d4e7f1a3 and no validation error occurred.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from https://code.vikunja.io/api.git, checks out the pinned v1.0.0 ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
